title: lichen_l2_iface_init: RX callback not cleared on late init failure
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 452-470

If `lichen_l2_iface_init()` fails after registering the RX callback (line 452) but before completing initialization (failures at lines 462 or 468), the callback remains registered via `lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback()`.

Current state after partial init failure:
- `iface_init_failed` = 1
- `link_ctx_initialized` = 1 (set at line 445)
- `lichen_iface` = iface (set at line 449)
- RX callback = lora_rx_callback (set at line 452)

What breaks: While packets are correctly rejected by the check at line 380 (`atomic_get(&iface_init_failed)`), the registered callback continues to run on every received packet, consuming CPU cycles to log the warning and return. More importantly, cleanup is incomplete - if a future re-initialization path is added, the stale callback state could cause subtle issues.

Fix: On init failure after line 452, call `lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(NULL, NULL)` before returning. Consider also clearing `lichen_iface` and `link_ctx_initialized` for consistency.
